zh.lifeSummary: 當Capt. Thomas S. Warner於1514年出生在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 沃爾坦時，他的父親Sir William Palgrave Warner29歲，母親Beatrice Hoe Warner30歲。他於1529年與Mrs. Thomas Warner在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 沃爾坦結婚。他去世於1546年4月23日在英國英格蘭艾可維坦享年32歲。

nameMeanings.name: Warner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements war(in) ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + heri, hari ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Warnier (Old French Garnier). Compare Garner and Werner .
English (of Norman origin): shortened form of Warrener (see Warren 2).

Irish (Cork): when this is not the Anglo-Norman name (see above), an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane ), found in medieval records as Iwarrynane, from a genitive or plural form of the name, in which m is lenited.

variants.name: Garner
meanings: English (of Norman origin): perhaps occasionally from the Old French personal name Garnier (see Garnier ), but it is exeptionally rare as a personal name in medieval England and no certain evidence has been found for its use as a surname. Compare Warner .

English: from Middle English gern(i)er, garner, gurner, Anglo-Norman French gerner ‘granary’ (Old French grenier, from Late Latin granarium, a derivative of granum ‘grain’). It was probably a metonymic occupational name for someone in charge of the stores kept in a granary.
English and Scottish: commonly shortened form of   Gardner  .

variants.name: Werner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Heinz, Gerhard, Ernst, Helmut, Horst, Klaus, Arno, Dieter.

German, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Hungarian, Norwegian, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + heri, hari ‘army’. The surname of German origin is also found in Britain (compare Warner ) and France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Verner .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Hungarian, Swedish, Danish, Croatian, and Slovenian   Verner  , a cognate or variant of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When Capt. Thomas S. Warner was born in 1514, in Great Waltham, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir William Palgrave Warner, was 29 and his mother, Beatrice Hoe Warner, was 30. He married Mrs. Thomas Warner in 1529, in Great Waltham, Essex, England, United Kingdom. He died on 23 April 1546, in Witham, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 32.

ko.lifeSummary: Capt. Thomas S. Warner(이)가 1514년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 월섬에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Palgrave Warner(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrice Hoe Warner(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1529년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 월섬에서 Mrs. Thomas Warner(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1546년 4월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 위섬에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Capt. Thomas S. Warner родился в 1514 г. в местности грит валтам, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir William Palgrave Warner было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrice Hoe Warner, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Thomas Warner в 1529 г. в местности грит валтам, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 23 апреля 1546 г. в местности витам, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 32 лет.
